question: [File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator call touchMarket with market.collateralParams[0].token set to the address of the Midnight contract itself, creating a market where the Midnight contract is a collateral token, and then fuzz whether supplyCollateral calls safeTransferFrom(address(midnight), ...) which would attempt to call midnight.transferFrom(), violating the invariant that collateral tokens must be standard ERC-20 contracts and not the core protocol contract, causing scoped impact: supplyCollateral reverts or creates unexpected state if Midnight has a transferFrom function? Proof idea: create market with collateral token = address(midnight), attempt supplyCollateral, assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket / isHealthy] Can an unprivileged market creator create a market with MAX_COLLATERALS (128) collateral entries all with LLTV_8 == WAD, and then a borrower activates MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) of them, and fuzz whether the isHealthy loop `while (_collateralBitmap != 0)` correctly terminates after exactly 16 iterations (since at most 16 bits can be set per the CollateralBitmap invariant), and whether the maxDebt accumulation overflows uint256 when 16 collaterals each have collateral[i] == type(uint128).max and price == ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE and lltv == WAD, violating the invariant that isHealthy must not overflow for any valid position, causing scoped impact: isHealthy reverts due to overflow, permanently blocking liquidation and collateral withdrawal? Proof idea: create 128-collateral WAD-LLTV market, activate 16 collaterals at max amount, call isHealthy and assert no overflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: setIsAuthorized (EcrecoverAuthorizer)] Can an attacker submit an Authorization with authorization.deadline == type(uint256).max, creating a never-expiring authorization signature that remains valid indefinitely, and then fuzz whether the nonce-based invalidation is the only protection against long-lived signatures, violating the invariant that authorization signatures should have a reasonable expiry, causing scoped impact: a leaked signature with deadline=type(uint256).max can be used at any future time to grant or revoke authorization, even years after the authorizer intended? Proof idea: sign Authorization with deadline=type(uint256).max, warp far into the future, submit, assert it still succeeds.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator call touchMarket with market.collateralParams[i].lltv == LLTV_7 == 0.98e18 and market.collateralParams[i].maxLif == maxLif(0.98e18, 0.5e18), and then fuzz whether the RCF computation for this near-WAD LLTV produces a maxRepaid value that is so large it effectively disables the RCF (since the denominator WAD*WAD - lif*lltv is very small), allowing full liquidation of any unhealthy position regardless of how small the undercollateralization is, violating the invariant that the RCF should limit liquidation to what is needed to restore health, causing scoped impact: borrowers in high-LLTV markets are over-liquidated even for tiny health violations? Proof idea: create LLTV_7 market with high cursor, create position 0.1% below health threshold, liquidate, assert RCF is correctly applied.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator call touchMarket with market.collateralParams[0].token == address(uint160(1)) and market.collateralParams[1].token == address(uint160(2)) (the two smallest non-zero addresses, correctly sorted), and then fuzz whether the collateral bitmap correctly maps index 0 to token address(1) and index 1 to token address(2), and whether a borrower who activates both collaterals has their health check correctly computed using the per-index oracle and LLTV, violating the invariant that collateral index must always correspond to the correct collateralParams entry, causing scoped impact: health check uses wrong oracle or LLTV for a collateral index, allowing undercollateralized borrowing? Proof idea: create market with two minimal-address collaterals, activate both, assert isHealthy uses correct per-index params.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: setIsAuthorized (EcrecoverAuthorizer) / touchMarket] Can an attacker who is authorized by a victim use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a malicious market-creator contract on behalf of the victim, and then the malicious contract calls touchMarket with a market where the enterGate is set to a contract that blocks the victim from exiting (violating the invariant that enter gates must not block exit), and then the malicious contract calls take on behalf of the victim to create debt in this market, violating the invariant that authorized operators should not be able to trap victims in malicious markets, causing scoped impact: victim's funds are locked in a market with a blocking exit gate? Proof idea: authorized attacker grants market-creator access, market-creator creates market with blocking gate, takes on behalf of victim, assert victim cannot exit.
